title: rubus nemoralis
intro: Rubus nemoralis, also known as the woodland bramble, is a species of wild raspberry native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of anthocyanins and other polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Rubus nemoralis include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
